name: boss-chamber
summary: The dungeon's final encounter room — large, theatrical, built around the boss's identity and fighting style. Space to maneuver, features to exploit, a silhouette to remember.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: generous — combat needs room, drama needs scale

accent.purpose: raised platform / arena circle / boss dais

primary_palette.note: lich or undead lord — throne among columns
cult leader mid-rite — the ritual itself is the battlefield
dragon's hoard — the boss sleeps on wealth
warlord's forge-hall — the boss fights with things they made
demon-summoner — the boss calls reinforcements from the pool

story_prompts: a vampire's hall — coffin at the apse, pillars casting long shadows
a collapsed cathedral where the boss fights beneath a broken dome
a dragon's cavern — stalactites above, hoard below, a central open floor
a hag's warren at the heart of a swamp-dungeon, many small alcoves
a warlord's audience hall turned battlefield, banners still hanging
an archmage's sanctum with three activated magic circles
anti_patterns: don't center the boss — offset to the back third so the party enters facing them across space
don't clutter the combat floor — pillars and features at edges, center stays open
don't underlight — drama needs visible silhouettes
avoid reusing a throne-room palette exactly; the boss chamber should feel more volatile
